title: Multi-Agent Research System
description: Build a research system where a coordinator agent manages: a web_searcher (searches the web), a data_analyzer (processes and synthesizes findings), and a report_writer (formats the final output). The system must handle hour-long research sessions, be resumable if interrupted, and produce structured reports. Data from searches can be very large — some search results return 20,000+ tokens.
architecture_notes: Sequential execution is required (search → analyze → report, each depending on prior). Persist agent state to external storage after each phase completion for resumability. Implement tool result trimming in the search tool handler — extract only relevant fields before appending to context. Use progressive summarization when conversation history exceeds 80% of context window, preserving all quantitative data verbatim. Implement structured error propagation: if data_analyzer fails, return typed error with retryable flag so coordinator can retry or reroute. Trace all calls with a shared trace_id for debugging.
key_patterns: tool result trimming before context append
progressive summarization with verbatim numeric preservation
